segments.text: Then the Lord appeared to him by the terebinth trees of Mamre, as he was sitting in the tent door in the heat of the day.
So he lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing by him; and when he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them, and bowed himself to the ground,
and said,
My Lord, if I have now found favor in Your sight, do not pass on by Your servant.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Please let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: And I will bring a morsel of bread, that you may refresh your hearts. After that you may pass by, inasmuch as you have come to your servant.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Do as you have said.
segments.speaker: The men

segments.text: So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah and said,
Quickly, make ready three measures of fine meal; knead it and make cakes.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: And Abraham ran to the herd, took a tender and good calf, gave it to a young man, and he hastened to prepare it.
So he took butter and milk and the calf which he had prepared, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree as they ate.
Then they said to him,
Where is Sarah your wife?
segments.speaker: The men

segments.text: So he said,
Here, in the tent.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I will certainly return to you according to the time of life, and behold, Sarah your wife shall have a son.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: (Sarah was listening in the tent door which was behind him.)
Now Abraham and Sarah were old, well advanced in age; and Sarah had passed the age of childbearing.
Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying,
After I have grown old, shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: Why did Sarah laugh, saying, 'Shall I surely bear a child, since I am old?'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Is anything too hard for the Lord? At the appointed time I will return to you, according to the time of life, and Sarah shall have a son.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But Sarah denied it, saying,
I did not laugh
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: for she was afraid. And He said,
No, but you did laugh!
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then the men rose from there and looked toward Sodom, and Abraham went with them to send them on the way.
Shall I hide from Abraham what I am doing,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: since Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: For I have known him, in order that he may command his children and his household after him, that they keep the way of the Lord, to do righteousness and justice, that the Lord may bring to Abraham what He has spoken to him.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Because the outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grave,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will go down now and see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry against it that has come to Me; and if not, I will know.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then the men turned away from there and went toward Sodom, but Abraham still stood before the Lord.
And Abraham came near and said,
Would You also destroy the righteous with the wicked?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Suppose there were fifty righteous within the city; would You also destroy the place and not spare it for the fifty righteous that were in it?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Far be it from You to do such a thing as this, to slay the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous should be as the wicked; far be it from You! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: If I find in Sodom fifty righteous within the city, then I will spare all the place for their sakes.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then Abraham answered and said,
Indeed now, I who am but dust and ashes have taken it upon myself to speak to the Lord:
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Suppose there were five less than the fifty righteous; would You destroy all of the city for lack of five?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: If I find there forty-five, I will not destroy it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Suppose there should be forty found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I will not do it for the sake of forty.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak: Suppose thirty should be found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I will not do it if I find thirty there.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Suppose twenty should be found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I will not destroy it for the sake of twenty.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak but once more: Suppose ten should be found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I will not destroy it for the sake of ten.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So the Lord went His way as soon as He had finished speaking with Abraham; and Abraham returned to his place.
